rulings.Case Title: JAMES CARMICHAEL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Loomis Equipment Company.¿S Motion For Summary Judgment Is Continued On The Court¿S Own Motion To February 8, 2011 At 9:30 A.M. In Department 220.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 8, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Loomis Equipment Company¿s motion for summary judgment is continued pursuant to C.C.P. § 437c(h) to March 3, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 220. It appears from plaintiff¿s papers that facts essential to justify opposition may exist but cannot now be presented, namely a deposition of defendant¿s person most knowledgeable. Substantive opposition, if any, to be filed and served by February 22. Reply, if any, to be filed and served by February 28. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES CARMICHAEL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Jacobs Constructors, Inc.¿S Motion For Summary Judgment Is Continued On The Court¿S Own Motion To February 8, 2011 At 9:30 A.M. In Department 220.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 8, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Jacobs Constructors, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 4 (negligence and premises owner/contractor liability) and off calendar as to issues 2, 3 and 5 (false representation, strict liability and punitive damages). Mr. Farley¿s declaration and Mr. Moser¿s deposition testimony create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing Durabla gaskets attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s request for a continuance is denied. Defendant failed to provide any justification for why it appeared at both days of Mr. Farley¿s deposition but did not ask him any questions. Plaintiff has dismissed his claims for false representation, strict liability and punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES CARMICHAEL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Calpacific Equipment Company¿S Motion For Summary Judgment Is Continued On The Court¿S Own Motion To February 8, 2011 At 9:30 A.M. In Department 220.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 8, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Cal Pacific Equipment Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing materials attributable to defendant. By failing to submit responses to contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant cannot successfully invoke the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Citing to and attaching plaintiff¿s responses to General Order No. 129 standard interrogatories is insufficient. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW THOMPSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Armstrong International, Inc.¿S Motion For Summary Judgment Is Continued On The Court¿S Own Motion To February 8, 2011 At 9:30 A.M. In Department 220.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 8, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Armstrong International, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing materials attributable to defendant. By failing to submit plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant cannot successfully invoke the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Citing to and attaching plaintiff¿s responses to General Order No. 129 standard interrogatories is insufficient. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW THOMPSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Air & Liquid Systems Corp.¿S Motion For Summary Judgment Is Continued On The Court¿S Own Motion To February 8, 2011 At 9:30 A.M. In Department 220.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 8, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Air & Liquid Systems Corp., successor by merger to Buffalo Pumps, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-4 (negligence, strict liability and sophisticated user) and taken off calendar as to issues 5 and 6 (false representation and punitive damages. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Further, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the packing and/or gaskets he removed from Buffalo pumps were original and that plaintiff knew of the hazards of asbestos when he performed the work at issue. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Moreover, proof that a plaintiff has knowledge of the safety hazards of asbestos is not, in and of itself, sufficient to successfully invoke the sophisticated user defense, at least where, as here, there is insufficient evidence that the plaintiff knew that defendant¿s product contained asbestos. Plaintiff represented that he dismissed false representation and punitive damages, although the court does not see that a dismissal has been filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW THOMPSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Moore Bros.¿ Motion For Summary Judgment Is Continued On The Court¿S Own Motion To February 8, 2011 At 9:30 A.M. In Department 220
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 8, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Moore Bros.¿ motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration, defendant¿s General Order No. 129 interrogatory responses and Mr. Ay¿s declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing gloves and blankets supplied by defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROGER ROSS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Gardner Denver¿S Motion For Summary Judgment Is Continued On The Court¿S Own Motion To February 8, 2011 At 9:30 A.M. In Department 220
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 8, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Gardner Denver, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Ross was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s separate statement fails to demonstrate that it propounded contention discovery and that plaintiffs¿ responses thereto were factually devoid. This precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Moreover, the deposition testimony and declaration of Mr. Ross and the declaration of Dr. Bruch create triable issues whether Mr. Ross was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant and whether that exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Ross¿ asbestos-related disease. Defendant¿s alternative motion for summary adjudication is off calendar since plaintiffs represent that they are withdrawing their claim for punitive damages, which is the only issue presented for adjudication. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 8, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Northrop Grumman Systems Corp.¿s demurrer to 2nd amended complaint is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed on January 14. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LUISA NICHOLS et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 8, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant United Technologies Corporation¿s demurrer to plaintiffs¿ second amended complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendant¿s general demurrer based on Bockrath is overruled as plaintiffs¿ complaint alleges facts sufficient to meet the Bockrath requirements. Defendant¿s demurrer is also overruled as to plaintiffs¿ fourth and fifth causes of action because plaintiffs¿ complaint alleges facts sufficient to state those causes of action. Defendant¿s demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to plaintiffs¿ second cause of action. = (220/HEK)
